text.title: Spec Section Rules and Traceability
text.summary: Required 14 sections, persona rules, user story format, and FR traceability chain requirements.

rule.statement: A full spec must include all 14 sections: Overview, Problem Statement, User Personas, User Stories, Functional Requirements, Non-Functional Requirements, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope, Assumptions and Constraints, Dependencies, Related Features, Recommended Skills, Open Questions, Appendix.
rule.rationale: Omitting any section creates a predictable gap: no Personas → generic user stories; no Out of Scope → scope creep in every session; no Assumptions → conflicting implementations.

rule.statement: Problem Statement must answer: Who (target user group), Problem (what they face), Impact (business or user cost), Solution (high-level approach), Success Metric (how to measure the feature succeeded).
rule.rationale: A problem statement without a success metric produces a feature with no clear done criterion. Agents and developers optimize toward undefined targets without it.

rule.statement: Write 2-3 personas with name, role, goals, pain points, and usage context. Reference personas by name in user stories and acceptance criteria.
rule.rationale: Named personas prevent "As a user" stories that apply to nobody specifically. Referencing them in AC forces scenario specificity.

antipattern.description: Persona defined in section 3 but never referenced in user stories or AC.
antipattern.reason: Orphan personas provide no filtering signal. If a persona is not referenced in at least one user story, either the persona is unnecessary or the user stories are too generic.

rule.statement: Every Functional Requirement must trace to at least one User Story (FR-X → US-Y). Every Acceptance Criterion must trace to at least one FR (AC-Z → FR-X). No FR may exist without a US parent. No AC may exist without an FR parent.
rule.rationale: Orphan requirements indicate either gold-plating (FR with no user story) or untestable design (FR with no AC). The chain ensures every requirement serves a stated user need and has a verifiable completion check.

example.code:
### FR-001: Add item to cart Traces to: US-001 (As Sarah, I want to add products to cart with one click)
**AC-001** (validates FR-001): Given Sarah is on a product page, When she clicks "Add to Cart", Then the cart count increments by 1 and a confirmation toast appears.
**AC-002** (validates FR-001): Given the product is out of stock, When Sarah clicks "Add to Cart", Then the button is disabled and shows "Out of Stock".

rule.statement: Every Out of Scope item must include: feature name, one-sentence reason it is excluded, and a "when" (Phase N, v2, never). Do not include items without all three fields.
rule.rationale: Items without explicit exclusion reasoning re-emerge in every planning session. The "when" prevents permanent exclusion from being confused with deferred scope.
